title: Wet Italian Anchovy Stracciatella
description: This sour, shaggy soup is a high-protein anti-flu dish that blends brine-wet anchovy fillets with creamy stracciatella pasta in a tangy broth.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat until shimmering.
Add the minced garlic and red pepper flakes, sautéing for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in the anchovy brine and bring to a gentle simmer, stirring to help the fillets dissolve.
Slice the anchovy fillets into thin ribbons and fold them into the simmering broth, cooking for 2 minutes.
While the anchovy mixture simmers, boil the spaghetti in salted water until al dente, then drain and reserve 1 cup of pasta water.
Add the cooked spaghetti to the anchovy broth, tossing to coat, and pour in a splash of pasta water to create a silky consistency.
Stir in the lemon zest and chopped parsley, adjusting seasoning with additional salt if needed.
Sprinkle the grated parmesan cheese over the top, letting it melt into the broth.
Serve immediately, optionally garnishing with extra parsley.
